title: Cross-process serve lockfile with PID liveness check
description: Per Gemini G2: PID lockfile without process.kill(pid,0) liveness check is silent deadlock liability post-SIGKILL. NEW services/serve-lock.ts: `.fabric/.serve.lock` PID file. Writers (serve startup, doctor --fix preflight, init --reapply preflight) read lock, check liveness via process.kill(pid, 0), on ESRCH treat as stale + auto-overwrite + log; on EPERM (process alive but not ours) refuse. --force CLI flag overrides. doctor preflight reports stale lock with recovery instructions. Removed by SIGTERM signal handler (TASK-014).
scope: Layer 2 P0 — concurrency protection for serve vs doctor --fix vs init --reapply

files.change: NEW: acquireLock(projectRoot) writes .fabric/.serve.lock with PID; readers stat() + process.kill(pid, 0); on ESRCH treat stale, log, overwrite; on EPERM throw IOFabricError lockedBy:pid; releaseLock(projectRoot) deletes file
Call acquireLock at startup; releaseLock at shutdown (also handled by signal handler)
Doctor preflight checks lock; if held by live PID warn + abort (or accept --force)
init --reapply preflight checks lock (hard error if held by live PID); --force override
NEW tests: stale lock recovery, live-lock refusal, --force override, fault injection (EPERM/EACCES)

criteria: Stale lock (ESRCH on kill -0) auto-recovered with log message
Live lock (kill -0 succeeds, PID != self) refuses with clear error including remediation
All tests pass in same commit
